intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1. Run a standard Seurat workflow in R: load, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; show me a UMAP and the top markers per cluster.

blocks.text: Good—I see **three** 10x samples here (GSM5746268, GSM5746269, GSM5746270), not just one. You asked for the GSM5746268 sample specifically. Let me load the Seurat recipe first, then present a plan for the workflow.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "seurat-scrna", "description": "Authoritative single-cell RNA-seq QC + clustering + cluster annotation with R/Seurat (v5).", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + clustering + annotation with R/Seurat v5 (standard PBMC3k workflow). Provision: ensure_capability('Seurat'), then library(Seurat). THE THREE CHOICES THAT DEFINE THE RESULT \u2014 surface them with present_plan before committing: (1) QC thresholds (subset cutoffs on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt); (2) number of PCs/dims (read off the ElbowPlot); (3) clustering resolution (FindClusters). Steps: ReadMtx -> CreateSeuratObject(min.cells=3,min.features=200) -> PercentageFeatureSet('^MT-') -> subset QC -> NormalizeData -> FindVariableFeatures -> ScaleData -> RunPCA -> FindNeighbors -> FindClusters -> RunUMAP -> JoinLayers -> FindAllMarkers. Load ggplot2 and dplyr explicitly; never library(tidyverse)."}

blocks.text: Now let me present a plan:

input.title: Seurat workflow: GSM5746268 QC, clustering, UMAP, and markers
input.summary: Load 10x matrix triplet, QC filter, normalize, PCA, cluster, UMAP, and identify marker genes per cluster.
input.rationale: Standard PBMC workflow with Seurat v5 — QC thresholds and clustering resolution are key decision points.
input.assumptions: <parameter name="item">Species is mouse (MGI gene IDs in the features file)
input.item: Using Seurat v5 layer-based idioms (not legacy)
